name: Finland Pandemrix childhood narcolepsy cohort (Nohynek)
denominator_method: All children living in Finland born 1 Jan 1991 - 31 Dec 2005, with vaccination status from the national vaccination registry and narcolepsy cases from national health registries.
time_zero_definition: Receipt (or non-receipt) of AS03-adjuvanted Pandemrix H1N1 vaccine during the 2009-10 campaign
funders: National Institute for Health and Welfare (THL), Finland
notes: Population registry-linkage cohort covering all Finnish children in the birth range, comparing narcolepsy incidence between Pandemrix-vaccinated and unvaccinated children (vaccine coverage 75%). Included to span the vaccine trigger class: the strongest example of an immune-mediated (autoimmune) post-exposure sequela with a defined denominator and an unvaccinated comparator. Of 67 confirmed cases, 46 vaccinated and 7 unvaccinated entered the primary analysis.

publications.title: AS03 adjuvanted AH1N1 vaccine associated with an abrupt increase in the incidence of childhood narcolepsy in Finland
publications.authors: Nohynek H, Jokinen J, Partinen M, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: narcolepsy incidence 9.0 per 100 000 person-years in vaccinated vs 0.7 in unvaccinated children (rate ratio 12.7)
